source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - South Pass Block 83

event.top_event: Laceration injury from falling PIG launcher cap
actions_taken: IP reported injury to HSE Technician at 1510 hours
Wound cleaned and bandaged on location
IP evacuated via helicopter at 1657 hours
IP transported to Occupational Medical Services in Houma, LA
event.summary: On 21 September 2023 at 1505 hours, an injured person (IP) suffered a laceration to the right hand while assisting two other operators in reinstalling a PIG launcher cap onto gas pipeline KZZ-602 on the cellar deck of unmanned Platform A, SP Block 83. The three-person crew removed the 12-inch cap for pipeline flushing operations. During removal, the lever arm assembly became damaged, forcing manual reinstallation. The crew mistakenly believed the cap was securely threaded after rotating it, and when they lessened their hold, the cap fell. The sharp edge of the lever arm shaft penetrated the IP's cut-resistant gloves, causing a laceration. The IP was evacuated by helicopter and treated at an onshore medical facility with eleven sutures.
recommendations: JSA should include sequential steps and guidance on preventing corrosion from seizing the cap onto the PIG launcher
JSA should establish safety measures to prevent personnel contact with sharp edges
Equipment such as chain hoist should be available and identified before project mobilization
Stop Work Authority (SWA) should be utilized when equipment is not available
JSA should be updated when equipment becomes damaged or unavailable
JSA should include manual handling approach for lifting, holding, and threading the cap

hazards.name: Sharp edges on PIG launcher cap lever arm

threats.name: Damaged lever arm assembly preventing mechanical cap installation
Inadequate securing mechanism for cap during manual handling
Inadequate communication between crew members during task

consequences.name: Laceration injury to hand from sharp edges
Loss of work time and personnel unavailability

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and establish safe work procedures

supporting_text: The JSA did not include sequential steps or guidance on how to prevent corrosion from seizing the cap onto the PIG launcher
The JSA also lacked guidance on how to eliminate or establish safety measures to prevent personnel from coming into contact with sharp edges
The JSA was not updated once the davit/swing arm was broken, which increased the challenges of the task and risk to personnel
The JSA was also missing a revision to identify a manual handling approach for lifting, holding, and threading the cap onto the PIG launcher

controls.name: Cut-resistant gloves
controls.barrier_role: Personal protective equipment to prevent laceration injury
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense against contact with sharp edges

supporting_text: the sharp edge of the lever arm shaft penetrated the IP's cut-resistant gloves
the sharp edge of the lever arm shaft penetrated his cut-resistant gloves and caused a laceration to the IP's right hand

controls.name: Mechanical cap securing device (davit/swing arm)
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent dropped object hazard
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering control to secure cap during installation

supporting_text: During the removal of the cap, the lever arm assembly that assists in positioning the cap on and off the launcher became damaged
This forced the crew to have to manually lift the cap back into place for reinstallation

controls.name: Chain hoist or lifting equipment
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to safely handle heavy cap during manual installation
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering control for safe manual handling

supporting_text: The contractor's tool house did not have equipment such as a chain hoist available, and the need for one was not identified before the project mobilization

controls.name: Stop Work Authority (SWA)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to halt unsafe work when hazards are identified
controls.lod_basis: Secondary administrative control to prevent unsafe task execution

supporting_text: personnel and leadership involved in the task failed to use the Stop Work Authority (SWA) when they recognized that equipment was not available
They did not halt the task until equipment or repairs could be afforded to secure the cap from becoming a dropped object
SWA should have been utilized once personnel recognized that the equipment to secure the cap from becoming a dropped object was not available on site

controls.name: Sharp edge mitigation or shielding
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent contact with sharp edges on cap lever arm
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering control to eliminate hazard at source

supporting_text: the sharp edges of the shaft of the pig launcher cap were not mitigated prior to performing the job, which would have shielded personnel from contact

the JSA should have included steps or guidance for corrosion seizing the cap, which would have helped to elevate the difficulty of removal and eliminate or establish control measures to prevent contact or shield personnel from sharp edges

controls.name: Crew communication and coordination protocol
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure coordinated safe task execution
controls.lod_basis: Secondary administrative control for task coordination

supporting_text: Lack of verbal communication and preparedness between the three workers before two of the three released their grip/hold

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
